title: trifolium lemmonii
intro: Trifolium lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's clover, is a perennial species of clover native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating coughs, fever, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Trifolium lemmonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
